sessions.details: recorded 1975-08-28 (one and only Peel Session). First TX (Top Gear) 1975-09-04.

track_listing.track: 'Fire (LP-Warhead)' (Atlantic)thumb|Solo LP from the former Jefferson Airplane chanteuse.

track_listing.details: LP-Welcome To The Wrecking Ball!; RCA

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there, this is TV on the Radio, here Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Right, tonight we return to the tried and trusted Friday Rock Show format.
Out of the archives, circa 1975, we feature Snafu, the band that contained Whitesnake member Mickey Moody.
And for the first time ever in BBC Session, we have Black Axe.
We've got some new stuff by Gillan, by Iron Maiden, by Rage, by B-Movie, by Aragon, by Moore, by Good Rats, and by Grace Slick.
Classics from the motels, Jeff Beck and Led Zeppelin, just to name a few,
and the return of the Friday Night Connection, the answer to the one we said three weeks ago, and a new one.
But this evening, we start with this.
That was Gillan, that was his new single, it's called New Orleans,
and it was specially for a gentleman by the name of André Chartouet.

Please, please, please.

Album called The Motels.

around a mate's house.

Lorna and Mike, who live in Battersea.
And now here's a really tasty single.
It's out on Neat Records.

And this is more.

The track was called Edge of the World.

The track you heard was the old Arthur Brown classic called Fire.
Four minutes exactly after 11 o'clock on the Friday Rock Show.
Time once again to revive our Friday Night Connection.
The one we set, the last one we set was on the 13th of February, right?
The tracks I played were Hammer Down from Free For All by Ted Nugent,

and Vice Versa by Samson from the album Head On.

It was either Hammer, Piledriver and Vice,
or Hammer, Paper and Vice, or rather Hammer, Plane and Vice.
You get it absolutely correct. It's been so long ago, I've nearly forgotten it.
Nonetheless, let me tell you that most everybody got it right.

or the one that appeals to us here at Broadcasting House.

The track was called On My Way to School.

Simon Sparks on lead guitar, Chris English on vocals,

and Stuart Richardson on the bass guitar.
Now, Stuart joined the band in about February of this year,
